content.claim: Hungary's 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks create a three-stage selection funnel that removes academically able students at ages 10, 12, and 14, earlier than the standard 14-year transition age in its nominally single-structure 8-grade system.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Describes mechanism but does not establish causal effects on equity or performance
Early selection at school entry (age 7) is also noted but not fully analyzed in provided notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
